title: Podman system prune deletes in-use CNI config
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

If we run `podman system prune -f --all` it deletes our `/etc/cni/net.d/10-calico.conflist` file and cri-o uses the fallback `100-crio-bridge.conf` CNI config. That shouldn't happen because calico is used and all pods are startet with calico as CNI provider. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install k8s cluster
2. deploy calico CNI 
```bash
kubectl apply  -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectcalico/calico/v3.24.5/manifests/calico.yaml
``` 
3. run the podman cleanup
```bash
podman system prune -f --all
``` 

**Describe the results you received:**
```bash
Nov 18 16:00:05 k8s crio[1378]: time="2022-11-18 16:00:05.588136899+01:00" level=info msg="Creating container: integration-pod/pod-755c688995-wf55w/httpd" id=6f383132-a27b-47ec-a5aa-107e1a2670ac name=/runtime.v1.RuntimeService/CreateContainer
Nov 18 16:00:05 k8s crio[1378]: time="2022-11-18 16:00:05.588350932+01:00" level=warning msg="Allowed annotations are specified for workload [io.containers.trace-syscall]"
Nov 18 16:00:05 k8s crio[1378]: time="2022-11-18 16:00:05.719839854+01:00" level=info msg="CNI monitoring event \"/etc/cni/net.d/10-calico.conflist\": REMOVE"
Nov 18 16:00:05 k8s crio[1378]: time="2022-11-18 16:00:05.725081687+01:00" level=info msg="Found CNI network crio (type=bridge) at /etc/cni/net.d/100-crio-bridge.conf"
Nov 18 16:00:05 k8s crio[1378]: time="2022-11-18 16:00:05.735002720+01:00" level=info msg="Found CNI network 200-loopback.conf (type=loopback) at /etc/cni/net.d/200-loopback.conf"
Nov 18 16:00:05 k8s crio[1378]: time="2022-11-18 16:00:05.735073751+01:00" level=info msg="Updated default CNI network name to crio"
``` 

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman should detect that calico CNI is used and NOT delete the CNI config file. 


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman --version
podman version 4.2.0
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No

on prem

Regards,
Kilian

comments.body:
This is the expected behaviour, Podman cannot know what configs are used by other tools. Most users want podman system reset or podman system prune do delete all networks so we did it anyway.

To fix it you can either switch podman to the netavark network backend or set a different network_config_dir in containers.conf.
